metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4969
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 2: Mouse primary cortical neurons were immunostained for MAP2, synaptophysin, and neuronal pentraxin-2 (NPTX2 / NP2), which showed partial co-localization.

Figure 4: High-resolution confocal microscopy images of triple immunostained cerebral cortical sections were segmented to identify individual C1q, NP2, and synaptophysin spots. NP2 (as well as NP1) showed extensive colocalization with both synaptophysin and C1q.

Figure 5: in biochemical subfraction, NP2 was enriched in synaptic cytoplasm subfraction (as opposed to NP1 which was enriched in synaptic membrane fraction).. Experimental description: Figure 4 legend: "Colocalization of synaptic C1q with NPs in mouse brain sections"

antibody:
Recombinant human His-tagged NP1 and NP2 proteins were purchased from R&D Systems (Minneapolis, MN, USA; catalog numbers: 7707-NP-050 and 7816-NP-050).

manufacturer website refers to human protein P47972 (Neuronal pentraxin-2) for antibody 7816-NP-050.

checks.label: OWL Consistency
checks.error_message: No inferences attributed to this model graph in the materializer output. Likely a parse failure or other materializer skip — re-run on this model alone to investigate.
